sec.method: HPLC of 0.22-um-filtered supernatant; dissolved STX release rate under control conditions (reported 'data not shown')

growth_records.cite: Vilar, Mauro C P et al. Ecophysiological Aspects and sxt Genes Expression Underlying Induced Chemical Defense in STX-Producing Raphidiopsis raciborskii (Cyanobacteria) against the Zooplankter Daphnia gessneri. Toxins (Basel) (2021). DOI:10.3390/toxins13060406. [PMC8230027]
growth_records.snippet: R. raciborskii T3 displayed a significant decrease in ... specific growth rate when exposed to Daphnia-conditioned medium compared with the control (mu_control = 0.47 +/- 0.15 day-1; mu_infochemicals = 0.24 +/- 0.04 day-1). ... Batch cultur
growth_records.notes: CONTROL condition. Measured specific growth rate mu = 0.47 +/- 0.15 day-1 (biovolume-based, first-order rate kinetics of log phase). Converted to per hour: mu = 0.47/24 = 0.01958 /h ~ 0.0196 /h. Doubling time = ln2/mu = 0.693147/0.47 = 1.475 day = 1.475*24 = 35.4 h (equivalently 0.693147/0.01958 = 35.4 h). growth_method marked 'calculated' because only the numeric per-day value is reported; the per-hour and doubling values are my unit conversions. Organism is a prokaryote (Cyanobacteria). Medium = ASM-1 (Gorham et al., cited ref [93]); full composition not given in text so medium_composition_stated=false. pH not reported. Secretion: dissolved (extracellular) total STX release rate 0.16 +/- 0.03 ug/L/day under control (paper states 'data not shown'); this is a measured excreted secondary-metabolite rate, not a central-carbon exchange, and no significant difference vs treatment. No substrate uptake rates reported (photoautotroph; C from CO2, N2-fixing). No mmol/gDW/h fluxes and no Yxs reported.

sec.method: HPLC of 0.22-um-filtered supernatant; dissolved STX release rate under predator-cue (infochemical) conditions (reported 'data not shown')

growth_records.cite: Vilar, Mauro C P et al. Ecophysiological Aspects and sxt Genes Expression Underlying Induced Chemical Defense in STX-Producing Raphidiopsis raciborskii (Cyanobacteria) against the Zooplankter Daphnia gessneri. Toxins (Basel) (2021). DOI:10.3390/toxins13060406. [PMC8230027]
growth_records.snippet: mu_infochemicals = 0.24 +/- 0.04 day-1 ... a significant decrease in ... specific growth rate when exposed to Daphnia-conditioned medium compared with the control (Student's t-test, p<0.05). Predator cues resulted in an STX release rate of
growth_records.notes: INFOCHEMICAL (Daphnia gessneri predator-cue) condition. Measured specific growth rate mu = 0.24 +/- 0.04 day-1 (biovolume-based, first-order rate kinetics of log phase); significantly lower than control (trade-off with toxin production). Converted to per hour: mu = 0.24/24 = 0.010 /h. Doubling time = ln2/mu = 0.693147/0.24 = 2.888 day = 2.888*24 = 69.3 h. growth_method 'calculated' = unit conversion from the reported per-day value. Secretion: dissolved (extracellular) total STX release rate 0.20 +/- 0.04 ug/L/day (paper states 'data not shown'), a measured excreted secondary-metabolite rate, no significant difference vs control (0.16). No substrate uptake rates (photoautotroph). No mmol/gDW/h exchange fluxes, no Yxs reported. Medium = ASM-1 conditioned with Daphnia infochemicals; composition not stated in text.
